sections.instruction: Complete the form below using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: Present address: Sea View Guest House, {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Questions 11-13: Complete the sentences below using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer. Questions 14-20: Complete the table below using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: The next meeting of the soccer club will be in the {{BLANK}} in King's Park on 2 July.
At the final dinner, players receive {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Complete the notes below using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 25-27: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 28-30: Which opinion does each person express about Box Telecom? Choose your answers from the box and write the letters A-F next to questions 28-30.
sections.passage_or_context:
Box Telecom
Options for Questions 28-30:
A its workers are motivated
B it has too little investment
C it will overcome its problems
D its marketing campaign needs improvement
E it is old-fashioned
F it has strong managers

questions.content: What does Karin think the company will do?
options: A look for private investors
B accept a takeover offer
C issue some new shares

questions.content: How does the tutor suggest the company can recover?
options: A by appointing a new managing director
B by changing the way it is organised
C by closing some of its retail outlets

questions.content: The tutor wants Jason and Karin to produce a report which
options: A offers solutions to Box Telecom’s problems.
B analyses the UK market.
C compares different companies.
A its workers are motivated
B it has too little investment
C it will overcome its problems
D its marketing campaign needs improvement
E it is old-fashioned
F it has strong managers

sections.instruction: Questions 31-36: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 37-40: Which section of the university will help postgraduate students with their dissertations in the following ways? Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to questions 37-40.
sections.passage_or_context:
Options for Questions 37-40:
A the postgraduate’s own department or tutor
B library staff
C another section of the university

questions.content: During the first week of term, students are invited to
options: A be shown round the library by the librarian
B listen to descriptions of library resources
C do an intensive course in the computer centre

questions.content: The speaker warns the students that
options: A internet materials can be unreliable
B downloaded information must be acknowledged
C computer access may be limited at times

questions.content: The library is acquiring more CDs as a resource because
options: A they are a cheap source of information
B they take up very little space
C they are more up to date than the reference books

questions.content: Students are encouraged to use journals online because
options: A the articles do not need to be returned to the shelves
B reading online is cheaper than photocopying articles
C the stock of printed articles is to be reduced

questions.content: Why might some students continue to use reference books?
options: A they can be taken away from the library
B they provide information unavailable anywhere else
C they can be borrowed for an extended loan period

questions.content: What is the responsibility of the training supervisor?
options: A to supervise and support library staff
B to provide orientation to the library facilities
C to identify needs and inform section managers
A the postgraduate’s own department or tutor
B library staff
C another section of the university
A the postgraduate’s own department or tutor
B library staff
C another section of the university

questions.content: checking the draft of the dissertation
options: A the postgraduate’s own department or tutor
B library staff
C another section of the university
